name: Abu Bakr ibn Hasan
title: Brother of Qasim
shortDescription: The teenage son of Imam al-Mujtaba (AS) and brother of Hazrat Qasim who entered the battlefield with unparalleled bravery and was martyred.
fullStory:
Abu Bakr ibn Hasan (AS) was one of the brave teenage sons of Imam Hasan al-Mujtaba (AS) who was present alongside his uncle, Imam Hussain (AS), during the event of Ashura. His and his brother Qasim's mother was a maid named Ramlah (or Nufaylah).

After the Imam's non-Hashemite companions (Ansar) were martyred one by one, it was the turn of the Hashemite youths. Abu Bakr, who like his brother Qasim considered death in the path of truth 'sweeter than honey,' went to the battlefield.

Despite his young age, he drew his sword and manfully attacked the enemy ranks. With the combat skills inherited from the Prophet's family, he took down a number of enemy forces.

Finally, after a brave battle, he was surrounded by Abdullah ibn Uqbah al-Ghanawi and, struck by an arrow or sword blows, attained the grace of martyrdom, joining his noble father in Paradise.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى أَبِي بَكْرٍ بْنِ الحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Abu Bakr, son of Hasan ibn Ali.
